parentQuestion: A person has gone for a deep-tissue therapeutic massage. Which of the following receptors would be stimulated by the massage?

scaffolds.explain: Unlike gentle Swedish massage, DEEP-TISSUE work uses SIGNIFICANT PRESSURE to reach deeper muscle layers. It can be uncomfortable or even painful.

wrongExplains.0: Deep-tissue massage specifically uses firm pressure, not gentle touching.
wrongExplains.2: Hot stones are a separate technique. Deep-tissue massage is about pressure.
wrongExplains.3: Massage definitely involves physical contact and pressure.

scaffolds.q: The 'hurts so good' feeling during deep massage suggests what?
scaffolds.explain: That intense, bordering-on-painful sensation comes from activating NOCICEPTORS (pain receptors). You're getting therapeutic pain.

wrongExplains.0: The 'hurts so good' sensation means nociceptors are definitely being activated.
wrongExplains.2: If it hurts, it's not too gentle — nociceptors are being stimulated.
wrongExplains.3: Thermoreceptors detect temperature. The pressure sensation involves nociceptors.

options: The simplest receptors — just bare nerve terminals
scaffolds.explain: FREE NERVE ENDINGS have no fancy capsules or structures. They're RAW NERVE TERMINALS that respond to intense stimuli. They're nociceptors.

wrongExplains.0: Free nerve endings are specifically UNencapsulated — bare nerve terminals.
wrongExplains.2: Free nerve endings are found throughout the body, especially in skin.
wrongExplains.3: Free nerve endings are sensory receptors, not blood vessel sensors.

scaffolds.q: What do free nerve endings (nociceptors) detect?
options: Painful, intense, or potentially damaging stimuli
scaffolds.explain: FREE NERVE ENDINGS (nociceptors) respond when stimuli are INTENSE enough to be concerning — strong pressure, extreme temperature, or tissue damage.

wrongExplains.0: Gentle touch activates encapsulated mechanoreceptors. Free endings detect intense stimuli.
wrongExplains.2: Free nerve endings detect potentially harmful stimuli, not pleasant ones.
wrongExplains.3: Free nerve endings detect multiple types of intense stimuli, not just cold.

scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES adapt rapidly — they fire when pressure STARTS and STOPS, but go quiet during SUSTAINED pressure. They're VIBRATION DETECTORS.

wrongExplains.0: Pacinian corpuscles adapt quickly and don't detect sustained pressure well.
wrongExplains.2: Body position is detected by proprioceptors. Pacinian corpuscles detect vibration.
wrongExplains.3: Blood sugar is monitored internally. Pacinian corpuscles detect mechanical vibration.

scaffolds.q: What do Golgi tendon organs detect?
options: Muscle tension from YOUR muscle contracting
scaffolds.explain: GOLGI TENDON ORGANS are in tendons and respond when YOUR MUSCLE contracts forcefully. They detect internal muscle tension, NOT external pressure.

wrongExplains.0: Golgi tendon organs detect internal muscle tension, not external skin pressure.
wrongExplains.2: Temperature is detected by thermoreceptors. Golgi organs detect muscle tension.
wrongExplains.3: Sounds are detected by cochlear hair cells. Golgi organs are in tendons.

scaffolds.q: What causes the intense sensation during deep-tissue massage?
scaffolds.explain: That uncomfortable-yet-therapeutic sensation is your NOCICEPTORS (free nerve endings) firing. You're essentially paying someone to activate your pain system in a controlled way.

wrongExplains.0: Golgi organs detect internal muscle tension. The massage pain comes from nociceptors.
wrongExplains.2: Photoreceptors detect light in the eyes, not massage pressure.
wrongExplains.3: Taste buds are in the tongue. Massage pressure activates skin nociceptors.

scaffolds.q: What does 'polymodal' mean for free nerve endings?
options: They only detect one type of stimulus
They respond to multiple types of intense stimuli
scaffolds.explain: Free nerve endings are POLYMODAL — they respond to intense mechanical pressure, extreme temperature, AND damaging chemicals. They're the body's GENERAL ALARM SYSTEM.

wrongExplains.0: Polymodal specifically means responding to MULTIPLE stimulus types.
wrongExplains.2: Free nerve endings definitely respond — to multiple types of intense stimuli.
wrongExplains.3: Polymodal refers to stimulus types, not receptor grouping.

scaffolds.q: Which receptor is most stimulated by deep-tissue massage?
scaffolds.explain: FREE NERVE ENDINGS (nociceptors)! The intense pressure of deep-tissue work is strong enough to activate these pain receptors. That's why it 'hurts so good.'

wrongExplains.0: Golgi tendon organs detect internal muscle tension, not external massage pressure.
wrongExplains.1: Pacinian corpuscles detect vibration, not sustained deep pressure.
wrongExplains.3: Muscle spindles detect muscle stretch, not external pressure causing pain.
